meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no portfolio. Learn to deploy a professional SaaS starter kit using Antigravity and Claude Code in this advanced developer guide.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends by shipping a production-grade SaaS starter kit. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for iterative development, you transform raw code into a high-fidelity, user-ready product. This workflow demonstrates technical mastery, providing concrete evidence of your ability to deploy scalable, real-world software applications.
content.intro:
The job market doesn't care about your GPA; it cares about your throughput. You are currently trapped in the 'tutorial hell' cycle, where your GitHub shows nothing but forks and abandoned exercises. This invisibility is the single biggest barrier to your career. You need a project that screams professional competence.

Today, we pivot. We aren't building a to-do list; we are architecting a modular SaaS foundation. By integrating Antigravity’s rapid scaffolding with Claude Code’s agentic logic, you will collapse months of development time into a single high-impact weekend. This is how you build a portfolio that forces recruiters to take notice.

steps.title: Architecting the Core Schema
steps.description: Define your database relationships and authentication flow before writing a single line of business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust SaaS schema using Supabase and Prisma, including multi-tenancy support, user roles, and subscription status tracking.

steps.title: Implementing Secure Auth
steps.description: Move beyond basic credentials; implement OAuth and session management that mimics enterprise-grade security.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure authentication middleware that handles JWT validation and protected route redirects for Next.js 15.

steps.title: Deploying the Stripe Integration
steps.description: A SaaS isn't real until it can accept payment. Integrate Stripe Webhooks to handle billing cycles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Stripe webhooks for subscription lifecycle events including checkout.session.completed and invoice.payment_succeeded.

steps.title: Building the Dashboard UI
steps.description: Focus on high-converting UX by building a responsive, themeable dashboard using Tailwind and Shadcn.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular dashboard layout with sidebar navigation, data table components, and loading skeletons.

steps.title: Automated Testing & QA
steps.description: Professional code is tested code. Automate your regression suite to ensure stability during updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Playwright end-to-end tests for the critical user path: signup to subscription checkout.

pro_tips: Always implement feature flagging from day one; it allows you to test new features in production without breaking the UX.
Use Claude Code to document your API endpoints as you build them; a documented repo is 10x more impressive to hiring managers.
Focus on 'Time to First Value' (TTFV) in your UI; users should reach the core functionality within 30 seconds of login.
Structure your commits using Conventional Commits to demonstrate professional version control habits.
Always include a 'README.md' that explains the 'Why' behind your architecture, not just the 'How' of the code.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live on Vercel, document the challenges you overcame in a blog post and link it directly to your GitHub repository.
content.conversion_block: You now have the blueprint to turn 'no experience' into a professional SaaS launch. Stop waiting for permission to build and start demonstrating your value to the world. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
